name: Marrakesh

palette.name: Medina Red Earth
palette.rationale: Red-earth walls and ramparts are the clearest architectural cue for Marrakesh's old city.

palette.name: Souk Saffron
palette.rationale: Saffron gold gives markets, spice displays, and warm craft lighting a useful accent.

palette.name: Menara Olive
palette.rationale: Deep olive green references historic gardens, groves, and planted courtyards around the city.

palette.name: Majorelle Blue
palette.rationale: Vivid blue is included as a cultural landmark accent associated with garden and museum imagery, not as a civic color.

palette.name: Riad Plaster
palette.rationale: Warm plaster softens the red city base for riad interiors, courtyards, and sunlit walls.

palette.name: Lantern Shadow
palette.rationale: Dark brown gives designers a shaded souk, wood, and metalwork anchor for contrast.

palette.name: Terracotta Gate
palette.rationale: Terracotta orange bridges monumental gates, clay surfaces, and late-afternoon medina light.

sources.title: Medina of Marrakesh
sources.note: Used for medina, ramparts, gardens, monuments, souks, and historic urban context.

sources.title: Marrakech
sources.note: Used for official tourism framing of city landmarks and visitor-facing identity.

notes: Complete starter record; all colors are editorial readings of architecture, gardens, markets, and atmosphere, not official city colors.
editorialSummary: Marrakesh's starter palette centers red-earth medina walls, then adds saffron, olive garden green, landmark blue, plaster neutrals, shadow brown, and terracotta for a warm urban heritage system.
